sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
Chief Complaint
Shortness of breath and dizziness.
History of Present Illness
The patient reports experiencing shortness of breath and dizziness approximately six hours after playing tennis in very hot and humid conditions. He described feeling like he was having a heart attack and sought emergency room evaluation. Since then, he has experienced lightheadedness and occasional dizziness. He reports being diligent with his heart failure medications and avoiding salty foods, though he occasionally cheats. He underwent cataract surgery three months ago and skin cancer removal five months ago, both of which have resolved without complications. He denies any vision problems or ongoing issues related to his skin cancer treatment. He has been closely monitoring his blood pressure, which was elevated in the emergency room.
Findings
Physical examination reveals a regular rate and rhythm heart, without murmurs. Lung auscultation reveals fine crackles bilaterally and trace lower extremity edema. Blood pressure is elevated. The ECG shows left ventricular hypertrophy. The echocardiogram indicates stable but slightly reduced left ventricular ejection fraction. Skin exam is unremarkable.
Assessment

sections.heading: Clinical Note
sections.section_value:
The patient's symptoms of shortness of breath and dizziness are most likely an exacerbation of his congestive heart failure, likely precipitated by dietary indiscretion and exacerbated by the heat and humidity. He has left ventricular hypertrophy contributing to his heart failure.
Plan
Prescribe an additional dose of lasix 80 milligrams once daily and carvedilol 25 milligrams twice daily. Schedule a follow-up appointment in one week to assess response to therapy. The nurse will administer the medications and provide patient education regarding dietary restrictions and medication adherence. Refer to physical therapy for cardiac rehabilitation.
